2606.12250.title: Reassessing High-Performing LLMs on Polish Medical Exams: True Competence or Bias-Driven Performance?
2606.12250.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) in medicine are mainly evaluated using multiple-choice question answering (MCQA), which can overestimate real clinical ability due to guessing strategies and answer biases. To address these limitations, we introduce an expanded and more challenging benchmark based on Polish medical exams, adding over 15,000 questions, two new domains, and four structural modifications that reduce MCQA-specific artifacts and better test reasoning. We evaluate 21 LLMs and show that evaluation design strongly affects results. Under our harder setup, the best model (Qwen3.5-122B) drops by 28.4 and 31 pp on English and Polish exams, respectively. Despite low evidence of data contamination, standard MCQA scores do not reliably reflect true medical competence. To facilitate further research, we make our benchmark publicly available.
2606.12250.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在醫學領域主要通過多選題回答（MCQA）進行評估，但這可能因猜測策略和答案偏見而高估真實的臨床能力。為了解決這些限制，我們引入了一個基於波蘭醫學考試的擴展且更具挑戰性的基準，新增了超過15,000道問題、兩個新領域以及四項結構性修改，以減少MCQA特有的工件並更好地測試推理。我們評估了21個LLM，並顯示評估設計對結果有很大影響。在我們更嚴格的設置下，最佳模型（Qwen3.5-122B）在英語和波蘭語考試中分別下降了28.4和31個百分點。儘管數據污染的證據較少，標準MCQA分數並不能可靠地反映真正的醫療能力。為了促進進一步的研究，我們將基準公開提供。
2606.12250.author: Antoni Lasik et.al.
2606.12250.authors: Antoni Lasik, Jakub Pokrywka, Łukasz Grzybowski, Jeremi Ignacy Kaczmarek, Gabriela Korzańska, Janusz Świeczkowski-Feiz, Oskar Pastuszek, Paulina Hoffman, Jakub Tomasz Dąbrowski, Wojciech Kusa
